introSection: Sky Rocket is a Premier Rides launched roller coaster located at Kennywood in West Mifflin, Pennsylvania, USA. Opened in June 2010, the ride occupies the spot formerly held by Turnpike near the entrance to the park.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The roller coaster was revealed in August 2009 around the same time that Turnpike was closed. The attraction was removed to make way for the new roller coaster and while Kennywood claimed it would be reinstalled, it has remained closed. The name Sky Rocket was revealed later that year in December. In January 2010, track pieces arrived at the park. Sky Rocket opened to the public on June 29, 2010 following a press preview the day before.

For the 2017 season, optional virtual reality headsets were added to Sky Rocket, with a $5 extra charge.

The ride was left standing but not operating from May 2018 to July 2019 due to a small electrical fire that damaged many components in the coaster's engine room. Among the damaged components was a generator that was melted down and had to be manufactured in Europe and shipped to the park. It reopened in July 2019 after a year of being closed.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Sky Rocket is the first roller coaster from Premier Rides to feature an LSM launch instead of an LIM launch.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains are open sided and riders are secured by lap bar restraints.

fullText:
Sky Rocket is a Premier Rides launched roller coaster located at Kennywood in West Mifflin, Pennsylvania, USA. Opened in June 2010, the ride occupies the spot formerly held by Turnpike near the entrance to the park.

The roller coaster was revealed in August 2009 around the same time that Turnpike was closed. The attraction was removed to make way for the new roller coaster and while Kennywood claimed it would be reinstalled, it has remained closed. The name Sky Rocket was revealed later that year in December. In January 2010, track pieces arrived at the park. Sky Rocket opened to the public on June 29, 2010 following a press preview the day before.

For the 2017 season, optional virtual reality headsets were added to Sky Rocket, with a $5 extra charge.

The ride was left standing but not operating from May 2018 to July 2019 due to a small electrical fire that damaged many components in the coaster's engine room. Among the damaged components was a generator that was melted down and had to be manufactured in Europe and shipped to the park. It reopened in July 2019 after a year of being closed.

Sky Rocket is the first roller coaster from Premier Rides to feature an LSM launch instead of an LIM launch.

The trains are open sided and riders are secured by lap bar restraints.
